title: Competitor wise — Competitor Capabilities by Value-Chain Segment
grain: one row per competitor company and value-chain segment (a company expands into several segment rows)
summary: Breaks down each competitor company by the insurance value-chain segment it addresses, with multi-line research notes describing the company's capabilities and offerings in that segment. It lets you profile a competitor's coverage across functions (e.g. underwriting, claims, policy administration) rather than by client.
use_cases: Which value-chain segments does a given competitor (e.g. Genpact) cover?
What capabilities does a competitor offer in a specific segment (e.g. Claims Management)?
Which competitors address a particular insurance function/segment?
Compare the breadth of segment coverage across competitors.

columns.name: Company Name
columns.note: competitor company; populated only on the first row of each company's block, so 87% null — forward-fill needed to attribute the segment rows below it to the correct company

columns.name: Segment - Co-Pilot
columns.note: insurance value-chain segment/function the competitor addresses (e.g. Underwriting, Policy Administration, Claims Management)

columns.name: CoPilot Research
columns.note: multi-line research notes describing the competitor's capabilities/offerings within the segment

examples.CoPilot Research:
Digital and AI-driven operations transformation
Enhanced broker management and submission handling
Solutions that strengthen submission-to-bind ratios

Data-driven underwriting using digital and AI technologies
AI-powered pre-bind underwriting automation
Use of specialized AI agents for classification, extraction, anomaly detection, and risk ranking

not_answerable.q: Which clients does this competitor serve in a segment?
not_answerable.reason: there is no client column on this sheet; it profiles competitors by segment, not by client

not_answerable.q: What is the FTE or revenue size of a competitor's segment presence?
not_answerable.reason: no size/headcount/revenue column exists on this sheet

not_answerable.q: How have these competitor capabilities changed over time?
not_answerable.reason: there is no date or time-series column; the table is a single snapshot

warnings: vintage_unknown — no date found in filename, sheet names, headers, or cell values; data owner should confirm the as-of date
Company Name is sparse (87% null): the company name appears only on the first row of each company's segment block, so forward-fill is required to associate segment rows with their company
